Description: Configure entities as API resources using PHP attributes for automatic REST/GraphQL endpoints
Do: Use #[ApiResource] with operations groups and filters on entities
Dont: Manually create CRUD controllers for each entity
Code Good:
#[ApiResource(
    operations: [
        new GetCollection(),
        new Post(),
        new Get(),
        new Put(),
        new Patch(),
        new Delete(),
    ],
    normalizationContext: ['groups' => ['post:read']],
    denormalizationContext: ['groups' => ['post:write']],
    paginationItemsPerPage: 20,
    order: ['createdAt' => 'DESC']
)]
#[ORM\Entity]
class Post {
    #[Groups(['post:read'])]
    #[ORM\Id, ORM\GeneratedValue, ORM\Column]
    private ?int $id = null;

    #[Groups(['post:read', 'post:write'])]
    #[ORM\Column(length: 255)]
    #[Assert\NotBlank]
    private string $title;
}

Description: Use API Platform's built-in filters for search ordering and filtering
Do: Apply filter attributes on entities. Use SearchFilter OrderFilter and DateFilter
Dont: Build custom query parameter parsing for filtering

Description: Use DTOs with State Providers and Processors to decouple API from entities
Do: Create Input/Output DTOs. Use State Providers for read and Processors for write
Dont: Expose Doctrine entities directly as API resources

Description: Control data exposure with serialization groups for different contexts
Do: Define read/write groups. Use different groups for list vs detail
Dont: Expose all entity fields in every endpoint

Description: Use Symfony Validator constraints directly on API Platform resources
Do: Apply Assert attributes on entity/DTO properties. API Platform validates automatically
Dont: Skip validation or validate manually in processors

Description: Test API Platform endpoints using ApiTestCase with built-in assertions
Do: Use ApiTestCase for type-safe assertions. Test status codes response format and IRI links
Dont: Test with generic HTTP client without API-specific helpers
